lines.en_lit: And Shmuel said: removing debris from on top of him, When of Shmuel was stated, it was stated with regard to the first If the majority are gentiles, he is a gentile. Shmuel said: But with regard to the matter of saving a life it is not so.
if a majority of gentiles a gentile. To what halakha Rav Pappa said: to feeding him non-kosher If a majority of Jews, he is a Jew. To what halakha to returning a lost object to him.
half and half a Jew. To what halakha Reish Lakish said: to damages. What are the circumstances? If we say that our ox, gored his ox, Let him bring proof and take
No, necessary when his ox, gored our ox, he gives him half, the other half, Let me say to Bring proof that I am not a Jew and take
one upon whom a rockslide fell, What is saying?

It is speaking Needless to say, Needless to say, uncertain he is there or not there, if he is alive, one clear But even uncertain alive or dead, one clear And needless to say, uncertainty alive or dead, a Jew, Rather, clear even if uncertainty he is a gentile or a Jew.
they found him alive, they remove If they find him alive, obvious No, necessary even live a short while

If dead, they leave him. this also obvious? No, necessary according to Rabbi Yehuda ben Lakish, as it was taught One not save a corpse from a fire, Rabbi Yehuda ben Lakish said: I heard that one save a corpse from a fire. Even Rabbi Yehuda ben Lakish said this only because a person is agitated over his dead If you do not permit him he come to extinguish However, here, if you do not permit him what might he do?

The Rabbis taught: until what point does one check until nose. And some say: until heart one checked and found the upper ones dead, he should not say: The lower ones are already dead, There was an incident where they found the upper ones dead and the lower ones alive.

Let us say that these tanna’im is like these tanna’im As it was taught From what point is the fetus created? from its head, as it is stated: “You are He Who took me [gozi] out of my mother’s womb” and it says: “Cut off [gozi] your hair, and cast it away” Abba Shaul says: from his navel, and he sends his roots in every direction

Even you say Abba Shaul, Until now, Abba Shaul spoke there only about formation, that everything is created from its middle; however, as for saving a life, even Abba Shaul admits that the main life is in the nose, as it is written: “All in whose nostrils was the breath of the spirit of life”

Rav Pappa said: The dispute from below, to above. But from above to below, once he checked as far as nose he is not required as it is written: “All in whose nostrils was the breath of the spirit of life”

It once happened that Rabbi Yishmael, and Rabbi Akiva, and Rabbi Elazar ben Azarya walking on the road, and Levi HaSadar and Rabbi Yishmael, son of Rabbi Elazar ben Azarya, walking behind them, This question was asked before them: From where that saving a life overrides Shabbat?

Rabbi Yishmael answered and said “If a thief be found breaking in Now, if this where there is uncertainty whether he comes money lives, bloodshed renders the land impure, and it causes the Divine Presence to depart from the Jewish people, is permitted to save himself at the cost of life, a fortiori saving a life overrides Shabbat.

Rabbi Akiva answered and said “And if a man comes purposefully upon his neighbor you shall take him from My altar, that he may die” from My altar” But not from on top of My altar. And Rabba bar bar Ḥana said Rabbi Yoḥanan said: They taught only in order to execute
